description: Registry of US election-data sources used (or usable) by the redistricting pipeline. Each source's `fetcher` is the script that downloads from it; null means manual / not yet automated. Required by `redist analyze --types political` (consumes tract-level CSV: geoid, dem_votes, rep_votes).

sources.name: Fekrazad reallocation (Harvard Dataverse)
sources.notes: Default for partisan analysis. CC0 license, redistributable. Single point of failure: dataset moves or retracts, this breaks. Methodology is precinct->tract area-weighted reallocation per Fekrazad's paper.

sources.name: MGGG OpenPrecincts
sources.schema: varies by state — each state's repo defines its own column names (e.g., G20PREDBID for 2020 Biden)
sources.notes: Most authoritative precinct-level source. Each state lives in its own GitHub repo (mggg-states/<STATE>-shapefiles). Requires precinct->tract reallocation by the consumer; we don't auto-fetch yet because state-specific schemas need per-state mapping.

sources.name: MIT Election Data and Science Lab — U.S. President 1976-2022
sources.notes: Hosted on Harvard Dataverse so the existing downloader's API mechanism works, but its file-pattern logic was written for Fekrazad's dataset structure (per-state CSVs named by state code). MIT EDSL publishes a single combined CSV per dataset, so the downloader needs an enhancement before auto-fetch is reliable. Useful for cross-validation of district-level metrics and for VAP-disaggregation (county results -> tract VAP).

sources.name: MIT EDSL — U.S. Senate Primary Returns
sources.schema: candidate-level rows; includes candidate name, party, votes
sources.notes: Primary-election data is critical post-Callais (Gingles 2/3 now requires controlling for party affiliation when proving racial bloc voting). County-resolution; needs disaggregation to tract for the new Callais bloc-voting analysis. Candidate names enable race-of-candidate annotation for within-party racial-bloc-voting analysis. **Look up the current DOI at electionlab.mit.edu/data before automating.**

sources.name: MIT EDSL — U.S. House Primary Returns
sources.schema: candidate-level rows; includes candidate name, party, votes
sources.notes: Primary-election data at congressional-district + county resolution. Same Callais-Gingles-2/3 motivation as the senate primary set. **Look up the current DOI at electionlab.mit.edu/data before automating.**

sources.name: OpenElections — precinct-level results
sources.level: primary | general | special (varies by state and cycle)
sources.notes: Authoritative open-source precinct-level archive. Each state lives in github.com/openelections/openelections-data-{STATE}. **Most useful single source for primary-election data at precinct resolution** — directly addresses Callais Gingles-2/3 within-party bloc-voting requirement. Auto-fetcher not yet written: requires per-state schema mapping. Cloning is straightforward (`git clone github.com/openelections/openelections-data-CA`); the dispatcher could be a thin wrapper.

sources.name: VEST — 2020 precinct shapefiles + results
sources.schema: VEST schema: G20PREDBID, G20PRERTRU, G20USSDxxx, etc.
sources.notes: Voting and Election Science Team — gold-standard precinct geometry + result joins for redistricting research. Per-state DOIs on Dataverse. Could be auto-fetched via the same downloader as Fekrazad/MIT (each state has its own DOI). Auto-fetcher not yet written because the per-state DOI list isn't centrally indexed; would need a sub-registry.

sources.name: California Secretary of State — primary precinct results
sources.schema: varies (precinct id, candidate name, votes); generally requires per-cycle parsing
sources.notes: Single-state exemplar — California publishes precinct-level primary results directly. Most other state SOS sites publish only county-level primaries. Useful as test data for primary-election analysis pipelines. Auto-fetcher not yet written: per-cycle URLs change, format varies. Manual download via the SOS prior-elections page.

sources.name: Princeton Gerrymandering Project — datasets
sources.notes: Research outputs supporting court submissions and academic publications. Not a primary data source but useful for cross-validation of Callais §2 evidence packages. Manual download per dataset.

sources.name: Dave's Redistricting App
sources.license: Proprietary; manual CSV export per the site's terms
sources.notes: Web app with precomputed precinct->tract reallocations. Manual export only — no public API. Useful when Dataverse data is missing for a state or when the Fekrazad reallocation is questioned.

sources.name: Daily Kos Elections — Presidential by Congressional District
sources.notes: District-level only, manually compiled. Duplicates MIT EDSL coverage at similar fidelity. Not a primary source for our pipeline; included for cross-checking.
